_comment: Claim-fidelity spec for the hitl skill: does a real `/charness:hitl` run honor its central claim — route to its chunk-presentation contract at the point of need? hitl has no single planner that emits required reads; RCF is derived from the SKILL.md workflow plus script-resolution analysis (lens 3). Of the references SKILL.md routes to, three are resolved by MANDATORY scripts run every representative session and so are engage-always but NOT RCF: adapter-contract.md (resolve_adapter.py resolves the adapter), and state-model.md (bootstrap_review.py materializes state.yaml/rules.yaml/queue.json and check_review_state.py gates every transition). rule-propagation.md is conditional (step 6 fires only when the user gives a stable rule) -> on-demand. report-mode.md is on-demand (report-packet input only). The one engage-always floor that no legitimate review run skips is chunk-contract.md: step 5 routes every bounded chunk presentation to it, the agent AUTHORS chunk content against it (check_chunk_contract.py is an optional post-hoc self-check, not a resolver), and the SKILL.md step 5 summary is a preview that defers to the doc as the authority -- the same shape as quality-lenses.md. The prompt is pinned to a representative bounded document review (hitl needs a target; bare /charness:hitl stalls), of an existing doc so the run reaches step 5 chunk presentation. charness ships a hitl adapter, so the run proceeds past the bootstrap gate into the loop. declaredReferences is the full set (coverage). referenceEngagement uses the engage-always/on-demand/gate-sufficient axis from the methodology contract (charness-artifacts/spec/2026-06-23-skill-claim-fidelity-doc-philosophy.md). BASELINE PROVEN 2026-06-29: the first live capture (charness-artifacts/cautilus/hitl-claim-fidelity-2026-06-29/) PASSED — a real /charness:hitl run opened references/chunk-contract.md and authored chunks against it, and crucially this planner-absent skill did NOT show the retro passive-pointer failure shape. Both retro and hitl use an explicit deferring pointer in SKILL.md; the difference is fakeability, not inlining -- retro's named-expert counterfactual is fabricable from general knowledge so its run skipped expert-lens.md, whereas hitl's chunk-contract output is repo-specific (disposition verbs, pseudo-tag rule, good/bad-chunk rubric, rewrite-review surface) and not fabricable from the step-5 preview, so the run had to open the doc (coverage 1/5, with the 4 unopened references exactly the script-resolved/on-demand ones the engagement map predicts). So hitl needs NO planner; the floor is live-capture proven, not a hypothesis. thresholds.max_duration_ms set from that PASSING baseline (101982ms) at ~2x headroom (240000), the retro model; runtime_budget_respect is a degrade signal, not pass/fail. SLICE 7 (2026-07-01) RCF→RSF MOVE — flaky doc-open retired, fidelity on the substance judge. A fresh capture (charness-artifacts/cautilus/hitl-claim-fidelity-slice7-2026-07-01/) scored outcome=FAILED, coverage 0/5: the run did NOT open chunk-contract.md, yet authored a faithful bounded chunk (Agent Assessment + non-binding Recommended Disposition + accept/revise/defer, materialized queue.json). This DISAGREES with the 2026-06-29 PROVEN capture (which opened it), so the doc-open is a NON-DETERMINISTIC proxy — a skill-shape signal (re-classify), not softened. Since hitl uniquely has a substance judge (outcome-assertions.json: chunk-shape, non-binding-disposition, stop-for-approval, materialized-queue), the floor moves off the flaky re-read proxy (requiredCommandFragments=[chunk-contract.md]) to the emitted-token floor requiredSummaryFragments=['recommended disposition'] (the chunk-contract presentation invariant, emittable from SKILL.md Output Shape) PAIRED with the substance judge — impl's form+substance split ('impl's closest twin', #410). The RSF label is also named in the prompt, so it is a weak FORM token on its own; the substance judge (non-binding-disposition + chunk-shape) is what actually grades fidelity and catches a hollow echo. chunk-contract.md stays DECLARED classTag DEPTH (rubric opened on-demand). thresholds.max_duration_ms=240000 retained (this capture 156085ms).
prompt: /charness:hitl docs/operator-acceptance.md 를 bounded human-in-the-loop로 청크 단위로 함께 검토하자 — 각 청크마다 원문 발췌 + Agent Assessment + 비구속 Recommended Disposition을 제시하고, 내가 승인한 뒤에만 다음 청크로 진행해줘.

adapter-contract.md.rationale: The bootstrap resolves the adapter via resolve_adapter.py on every run (artifact location, chunk target, apply mode), so adapter readiness is unconditional; but the run reads the resolved adapter output, not this explainer doc, so it is engage-always yet not an RCF floor (lens 3, script-resolved).

chunk-contract.md.rationale: Step 5 routes every bounded chunk presentation here, and the agent AUTHORS each chunk (original-material excerpt, related context, Agent Assessment, non-binding Recommended Disposition, decision) against this contract; check_chunk_contract.py is an optional post-hoc self-check, not a resolver, so a representative review that never opens this is not following its own routing. SLICE 7 re-classification: the chunk-contract.md doc-open is a FLAKY floor — the 2026-06-29 capture opened it (coverage 1/5) but the 2026-07-01 re-capture did NOT (coverage 0/5) while still authoring faithful chunks (Agent Assessment + non-binding Recommended Disposition + accept/revise/defer), because the chunk-presentation shape is inlined in SKILL.md step 5 + Output Shape. So the doc-open is not reliably forced; it is a DEPTH ref opened on-demand for the rubric (good/bad-chunk, pseudo-tag, rewrite-review), not an RCF floor. Fidelity moves to the emitted-token floor + the existing substance judge.

report-mode.md.rationale: Step 10 routes here only for generated report packets; a typical bounded code/document review presents chunks per chunk-contract.md without a report packet.
report-mode.md.trigger: when the input is a generated report packet with structured items and decision cards rather than a branch diff or document review

rule-propagation.md.rationale: Step 6 fires only when the user gives a stable, reusable rule to propagate across remaining chunks; a representative accept/revise review without a reusable rule never engages it (the prior engage-always classification mislabeled a conditional step).
rule-propagation.md.trigger: when the user gives a stable rule that should apply to remaining chunks, or a chunk edit must read accepted rules into active_rules_applied

state-model.md.rationale: Steps 3 and 7 depend on resumable state, but bootstrap_review.py materializes the canonical state files (state.yaml/rules.yaml/queue.json) and check_review_state.py gates every pre-edit/cursor-advance transition, so the run drives the loop from the scripts' created files and gate errors rather than the explainer doc: engage-always yet not an RCF floor (lens 3, script-resolved).
